implementation_summary: Migrated the five existing themes (calm, playful, lego, minecraft, flower) out of app/src/lib/theme.ts's inline createTheme() calls into pure-data ThemeManifest objects under app/src/themes/<id>/manifest.ts, transcribed field-for-field from theme.ts's createTheme() inputs (colors, typography, radii, backgroundImage) plus useThemeStyles.ts's per-visualStyle treatment blocks (card/button/nav elevation, surface pattern, contentPanel, icon set) and BANNER_SVG. Authored a hand-written registry.generated.ts stub matching the exact shape the S3 generator will later emit (static manifest imports, folder-derived AppThemeName union, order-sorted APP_THEMES, buildThemeFonts-deduped THEME_FONTS). Rewired themes/index.ts from S1's no-op pass-through into the real public API: getChakraTheme(id) now bridges id -> registry manifest -> buildChakraTheme.ts's manifest-keyed getChakraTheme (aliased to avoid a name collision), and getThemeDefinition(id)/APP_THEMES return a transitional AppThemeDefinition (ThemeManifest plus four @deprecated derived compat fields: visualStyle, labelKey, descriptionKey, backgroundImage) so the three S4-scheduled, untouched consumers (useThemeStyles.ts, ThemeIcon.tsx, profile.tsx) keep compiling and behaving identically. Flipped lib/theme.ts to a thin re-export and types/index.ts's AppThemeName to an import from the registry.
compat_bridge_decision: AppThemeDefinition = Omit<ThemeManifest,'id'> & { id: AppThemeName; visualStyle: ThemeVisualStyle; labelKey; descriptionKey; backgroundImage? }, all four extra fields derived (never independently authored) from the manifest: visualStyle from treatments.card via a hand-maintained ELEVATION_TO_VISUAL_STYLE reverse map; labelKey/descriptionKey from id (id already equals the historical i18n key); backgroundImage from colors.backgroundImage. contentSurface required NO derivation — S1's schema.ts already places it as a top-level optional boolean on ThemeManifest itself, matching the pre-refactor field exactly (no 'behavior' nesting exists in this codebase). Each field carries an @deprecated JSDoc naming its S4 removal condition. Full rationale is in architecture.json's design_notes (also covers why registry.generated.ts's own APP_THEMES and themes/index.ts's re-exported APP_THEMES are deliberately different shapes under the same name, and why that does not collide).

verification_summary.ac_parity_1_evidence: app/tests/unit/themes/parity.test.ts's 5 it.each(...) cases deep-equal pick(getChakraTheme(id), ALLOWLIST) against the pre-existing, S0-committed app/tests/unit/theme-baseline.generated.ts via toEqual (never toStrictEqual, per that file's own header) — genuine comparison against the frozen pre-refactor fixture, not a re-derived expectation. All 5 pass. The full-extend guard (components.Button.variants non-empty) also passes for all 5.
verification_summary.ac_parity_2_evidence: getThemeDefinition('minecraft').contentSurface === true; calm/playful/lego/flower falsy. getChakraTheme('minecraft').fontSizes: md=1.12rem (>=1.1), lg=1.25rem (>md), 4xl=2.1rem (>xl=1.35rem). All assertions pass.
verification_summary.lint: NO_LINT (unchanged since S0/S1 — no ESLint config present in the repo)

retrospective.harder_than_needed: Two design decisions required cross-referencing architecture.md against the story brief's own explicit override rather than following either alone: (1) the story brief's prose described the compat bridge as needing a 'nested behavior.contentSurface' field, but S1's already-committed schema.ts places contentSurface as a top-level optional boolean on ThemeManifest — no 'behavior' object exists anywhere in this codebase's actual schema, so the correct move was to recognize the brief's phrasing as generic/illustrative and defer to the real, already-frozen S1 schema rather than introduce a nesting that isn't there. (2) architecture.md's Registry seam contract states the terminal 'AppThemeDefinition = ThemeManifest' alias, which appears to conflict with the story brief's explicit direction to add transitional compat fields; resolving this required treating architecture.md's line as describing the S4 end-state (once the three unmigrated consumers no longer need those fields) rather than a same-story constraint, and documenting that reading explicitly in architecture.json so a reviewer isn't left wondering why the story diverges from the letter of the seam contract.
retrospective.surprised_by: profile.tsx reads labelKey/descriptionKey/backgroundImage directly off every entry in Object.values(APP_THEMES) (not just off getThemeDefinition's single active-theme return) — meaning the compat augmentation had to apply to the whole exported APP_THEMES map, not only to getThemeDefinition's return value, which the story brief's RISK section didn't call out explicitly (it only named useThemeStyles.ts/ThemeIcon.tsx's activeThemeDefinition.visualStyle/.contentSurface reads). Missing this would have produced a TS compile error and a broken theme picker on /profile.
